event: presented to the infectious diseases clinic
lived in Guatemala for five years
HIV-antibody test done by ELISA and sputum AFB smear were positive
HIV-1 Western blot test was done and the result was positive
fluconazole and trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole were given as prophylaxis
Chest X-ray showed costophrenic angle blunting and fluid shifting in the right hemithorax and mild pneumonic infiltration in left lung
Mycobacterium tuberculosis was identified from his sputum cultured at another hospital in Seoul
rifampin, zidovudine, and trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole were discontinued
developed new pulmonary infiltrates and septic shock
empirical antibiotic therapy with piperacillin/tazobactam was started
transferred to the intensive care unit for mechanical ventilator support
Gram stain and ordinary culture from sputum and blood revealed no pathologic organisms
pneumonia, and hepatosplenomegaly did not improve
bone marrow aspiration and biopsy were performed
died as a result of refractory septic shock and hypoxia
results of the bone marrow aspiration smears and biopsy revealed numerous intra-cytoplasmic organisms identified as Histoplasma capsulatum
